qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: PathPrefix route matching accepts /../ dot segments and bypasses protected middleware
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets Traefik v1 PathPrefix route selection in rules.go/server_configuration.go, while the advisory is a distinct v2/v3 StripPrefixRegex Path/RawPath desynchronization in pkg/middlewares/stripprefixregex/strip_prefix_regex.go.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding is a Traefik v1 PathPrefix raw-path routing bypass using /../ normalization. The advisory describes a different root cause: StripPrefixRegex in Traefik v2/v3 matches decoded Path but slices encoded RawPath, producing a misleading dot-segment path for ForwardAuth/BasicAuth/DigestAuth.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe StripPrefixRegex middleware desynchronizing req.URL.Path and req.URL.RawPath and cite pkg/middlewares/stripprefixregex/strip_prefix_regex.go as the vulnerable code path.
The finding report instead cites repo/rules/rules.go and repo/server/server_configuration.go and explains a PathPrefix strings.HasPrefix match on /foo/../admin selecting the wrong frontend.

Both issues are auth bypasses in Traefik, but they rely on different components, different versions, and different vulnerable control flow, so the finding does not identify the advisory-described root cause.
